\needspace{4\baselineskip}
\section{Brown Butter Chocolate Chip Cookies}
\recipemeta{24 servings · 20 minutes prep · 12 minutes cook}
\recipedescription{Crispy edges, chewy centers, and complex nutty flavor from brown butter. Worth the extra step.}
\subsection*{Ingredients}
\begin{ingredients}
\item \textbf{butter} -- 3/4 cup, 1.5 sticks
\item \textbf{brown sugar} -- 3/4 cup, packed
\item \textbf{granulated sugar} -- 1/2 cup
\item \textbf{eggs} -- 2, large
\item \textbf{vanilla extract} -- 2 tsp
\item \textbf{all-purpose flour} -- 2 cups
\item \textbf{baking soda} -- 1 tsp
\item \textbf{salt} -- 1 tsp, flaky sea salt
\item \textbf{chocolate chips} -- 12 oz, mix of dark and milk chocolate
\item \textbf{flaky sea salt}
\end{ingredients}
\subsection*{Instructions}
\subsubsection*{Brown the butter}
\begin{enumerate}
\item In a saucepan, melt butter over medium heat. Continue cooking, stirring frequently, until butter turns golden brown and smells nutty, about 5 minutes.
\item Transfer browned butter to a large bowl and let cool for 10 minutes.
\end{enumerate}
\subsubsection*{Make the dough}
\begin{enumerate}
\item Add brown sugar and granulated sugar to the cooled butter. Whisk until combined.
\item Add eggs and vanilla extract, whisking until smooth.
\item In a separate bowl, whisk together all-purpose flour, baking soda, and salt.
\item Fold dry ingredients into wet ingredients until just combined. Fold in chocolate chips.
\end{enumerate}
\subsubsection*{Bake}
\begin{enumerate}
\item Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Line baking sheets with parchment paper.
\item Scoop dough into 2 tablespoon-sized balls and place on prepared baking sheets, spacing them 3 inches apart.
\item Bake for 11-13 minutes until edges are golden brown but centers still look slightly underdone.
\item Let cool on baking sheet for 5 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack.
\item Sprinkle with extra flaky sea salt while still warm, if desired.
\end{enumerate}
\recipeattribution{Recipe by Claire Saffitz}
\vspace{3\baselineskip}
